issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState() does not update aria-pressed on period buttons

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() lacks live-region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates visible prices but does not announce the change or new prices to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the financial impact.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4578: After requestAnimationFrame(() => { _pricingState.animating = false; }); add const periodLabel = BILLING_PERIODS[period].label; liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${periodLabel} billing`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid

issuesFound.fix: Line 2790: After the if block handling aria-describedby, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller shows and hides panel elements by removing or adding the hidden attribute, but fails to update aria-expanded on the trigger button to indicate the dropdown state. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded/collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter not announcing result count changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the visible result count updates in the DOM but no live region announcement is made to inform screen reader users how many products match the selected filters.

issuesFound.explanation: While the close function sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger, the open function fails to set aria-expanded="true" when expanding an FAQ item. This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: pricing not announcing billing period changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers to indicate the change in billing period and prices.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons not updating aria-pressed

issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons are not initialized with aria-pressed attributes to indicate which period is selected by default.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but omits the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. The trigger's initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element configured as a combobox lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute, preventing screen readers from knowing the suggestion list is initially collapsed.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error message to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks aria-labelledby referencing its visible heading, preventing easy navigation to this landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1518: Add aria-labelledby="hero-heading" to the section element: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it does not communicate this busy state to assistive technology via aria-busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1070: Add aria-busy={isLoading} to the button element: `<button aria-busy={isLoading} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component not exposing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop to style the field but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the error state of the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 889 (approximate): Add aria-invalid to the input: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} className={...} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a sub-menu using the isOpen state but does not expose this state to assistive technology. Screen-reader users cannot tell whether the sub-menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1076 (approximate): Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" ...>`

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen-reader users may encounter redundant or confusing spinner text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 843 (approximate): Add aria-hidden to the spinner: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons act as a toggle group where one is always active, but they lack aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state. Screen-reader users cannot tell which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1276 (approximate): Add aria-pressed to the button: `<button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} ...>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger buttons inside the mobile navigation control expandable sub-menus using isExpanded state but lack aria-expanded. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the expand/collapse state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154 (approximate): Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID ('newsletter-email-error') via aria-describedby when invalid, but FormGroup is not passed the corresponding errorId prop, so the element does not exist in the DOM. Screen-reader users cannot access the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1768 (approximate): Pass errorId to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button is icon-only (contains only an emoji) and the required aria-label prop is not provided. Screen-reader users hear only "button" with no context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1219 (approximate): Add aria-label={LABELS[theme]} to IconButton: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} ...>`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is styled visually but lacks aria-current="page". Screen-reader users cannot programmatically identify which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1089 (approximate): Add aria-current to the current link: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ disclosure button toggles answer visibility using isOpen state but does not communicate this state via aria-expanded. Screen-reader users cannot tell if the answer is shown or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1684 (approximate): Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile menu is styled visually but lacks aria-current="page". Screen-reader users cannot identify the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1168 (approximate): Add aria-current to the current link: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a major page region but lacks aria-labelledby or aria-label. Screen-reader users navigating by landmarks cannot identify the purpose of this section.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244 (approximate): Add aria-labelledby to the section: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel is icon-only (contains only "✕") and lacks an aria-label. Screen-reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144 (approximate): Add aria-label to IconButton: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} ...>`
